title: HashMap<String,Value> argument extraction pattern duplicated across every tool
description:
## Severity
Major

## Dimension
DRY / KISS / SOLID-S

## Location
src/tools/file/write.rs:9-40, src/tools/file/edit.rs:9-66, src/tools/file/notebook.rs:49-78, src/tools/file/mod.rs:188-198, src/tools/cron.rs:142-161, src/tools/todo.rs:76-127, src/tools/worktree.rs:59-68

## Violation
Every executor reimplements identical "extract a required string arg or return an error string" logic, e.g. cron.rs:142-161:

```rust
let name = match args.get("name").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {
    Some(n) => n.to_string(),
    None => return ("Error: name is required".to_string(), true),
};
let cron_expression = match args.get("schedule").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) { ... };
let prompt = match args.get("prompt").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) { ... };
```

Same pattern in todo.rs:88-121, notebook.rs:50-75, etc. The error message style even drifts between files ("Error: name is required" vs "Missing 'path' argument" vs "missing 'content' field"). This is the textbook DRY violation: N copies of the same dispatch protocol, each free to regress independently.

## Mandated refactor
Introduce a typed `ToolArgs` extractor (use `serde` deserialization into a per-tool input struct; e.g. `#[derive(Deserialize)] struct CronCreateArgs { name: String, schedule: String, prompt: String, ... }`). Replace the manual extraction blocks with `let args: CronCreateArgs = serde_json::from_value(raw_args.into())?;`. This gives uniform error messages, free defaults via `#[serde(default)]`, schema-level rejection of unknown fields, and eliminates the duplicate validation code entirely.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
